title: USING_* constants eliminate per-call Vec allocation and prevent capability bugs
description: build_request() now takes an explicit &[&str] capability list via the pre-defined USING_CHAT, USING_QUOTAS, USING_CORE constants. A contributor might suggest a simpler approach (e.g., always sending all capabilities, or using an enum). The current design is correct: (1) RFC 8620 §3.3 requires that ONLY the capabilities needed by the request's methods are declared — sending extra capabilities is a spec violation that causes servers to return unknownCapability; (2) the body_json tests in batch_test.rs and methods_test.rs verify the exact capability array, catching regressions immediately; (3) constants prevent per-call string literal typos that would silently send wrong URIs. Do not simplify to 'always send all capabilities'.
close_reason: Decision record: current implementation is correct; no code change needed
